nameMeanings.name: Devereux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, Sean.
English and Irish: of Norman origin, a habitational name for someone from Évreux in Eure, France, with the preposition d(e). See also Everest for the name without the preposition.

History: This was the surname of the Earls of Essex from 1572 to 1641. Walter Devereux, 1st Earl of Essex, led an attempted colonization of Ulster, and his son, Robert, 2nd Earl of Essex, briefly held the position of lord lieutenant of Ireland in 1599.

variants.name: Devereaux
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, a variant of   Devereux  .

variants.name: Everest
meanings: English (southeastern): of Norman origin, a variant of Devereux without the fused preposition (Old French de). Compare Evers .

variants.name: Evers
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of Devereux without the fused preposition (Old French de).

English: de-aspirated variant of Heaver, with post-medieval excrescent -s, a habitational name from Hever (Kent), named from Old English hēah ‘high’ + (Kentish) Old English ēfre ‘edge or brow of a hill’. Alternatively, the name may derive from some minor place in southeastern England named with Old English ȳfre, (Kentish) Old English ēfre, Middle English evere ‘edge or brow of a hill, tip of a promontory’, with the addition of a prosthetic H-.
North German and Dutch: genitivized patronymic from the personal name Evert , which lost its t in pronunciation and spelling subsequently; compare Everts . Compare also Avers .

variants.name: Deveraux
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, a variant of   Devereux  .

variants.name: Devery
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Kieran, Brendan, Clancy.
Irish (Offaly): of Norman origin, a variant of   Devereux  .

variants.name: Devary
meanings: Probably Irish: variant of Devery, a variant of Devereux in counties Leix and Offaly.

variants.name: Debrow
meanings: Altered form of English or Irish   Devereux  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John I Devereux was born in 1302, in Hereford, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Walter Devereux, was 41 and his mother, Margaret De Braose, was 40. He married Elizabeth De La Bern about 1320. He died on 22 February 1393, in London, Middlesex, England, at the age of 91, and was buried in City of London, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Elizabeth De La Bern environ 1320. Il est décédé le 22 février 1393, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 91 ans, et a été enterré à City of London, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John I Devereux(이)가 1302년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 허퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter Devereux(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret De Braose(은)는 40세였다. 그는 대략 1320년 Elizabeth De La Bern(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1393년 2월 22일 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 91세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 시티 프 런던에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John I Devereux родился в 1302 г. в местности хирефорд, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Walter Devereux было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Margaret De Braose, 40. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth De La Bern около 1320 г. Он умер 22 февраля 1393 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 91 год и был похоронен в местности сити оф лондон, Англия, Великобритания.
